context: Tests whether the agent uses the elixir-skill-router's bug-fix skill chain correctly: routing to testing-essentials first, enforcing the GATE that a reproduction test must fail before implementation, sequencing elixir-essentials after the gate, and never producing Elixir implementation code itself.

checklist.name: First line routing
checklist.description: The very first substantive line of routing_response.md is exactly 'Next skill: skills/testing/testing-essentials' (or equivalent canonical path format pointing to testing-essentials)

checklist.name: testing-essentials first
checklist.description: The response routes to testing-essentials as the first skill in the chain (before any other skill)

checklist.name: Explicit GATE condition
checklist.description: The response explicitly states a gate or blocking condition that must be met before moving to the implementation step — the reproduction test must exist and fail (not just 'write a test')

checklist.name: Test must fail (not pass)
checklist.description: The gate condition specifies that the reproduction test must FAIL (i.e., confirm the bug is reproducible) — not simply exist or pass

checklist.name: elixir-essentials in chain
checklist.description: The skill chain includes elixir-essentials as the implementation step after the testing gate

checklist.name: elixir-essentials after gate
checklist.description: elixir-essentials appears after testing-essentials (and after the gate) in the ordered chain, not before it

checklist.name: Verify/confirm step
checklist.description: The chain ends with a verification or confirmation step (e.g., verify the fix passes, re-run tests, or 'verify passes')

checklist.name: No Elixir implementation code
checklist.description: routing_response.md does NOT contain any Elixir source code (no .ex/.exs snippets, no def/defmodule/defp blocks, no pattern match or case/with blocks written as implementation)

checklist.name: Triage not direct fix
checklist.description: The response frames itself as a routing plan or triage, not as a direct code solution — it delegates to skills rather than implementing the fix

checklist.name: Priority rule stated
checklist.description: The response includes a priority or chain statement (e.g., 'Priority: TDD → ...' or 'Chain: testing-essentials then elixir-essentials') when more than one skill is referenced
